LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., AND THE HANOVER INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR UNDERTAKING FROM OUT OF STATE AND FOREIGN CORPORATION CI ENERGIA SOLAR S.A.: DEFENDANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC.'S MOTION FOR UNDERTAKING IS DENIED. BAGATELOS FAILS TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT THERE IS A "REASONABLE POSSIBILITY" THAT IT WILL PREVAIL WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1030(A). THE ONLY EVIDENCE THAT BAGATELOS RELIES UPON IS THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL CHRISTOPHER RODRIGUEZ. THE COURT SUSTAINS C.I. ENERGIA'S OBJECTIONS TO THAT DECLARATION. THE DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW THAT MR. RODRIGUEZ HAS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE REGARDING EXHIBITS C THROUGH F. IN ADDITION, THERE IS NO ANALYSIS AS TO HOW THE ATTACHED DOCUMENTS SHOW THAT C.I. ENERGIA PROVIDED A DEFECTIVE PRODUCT SUCH THAT BAGATELOS WILL PREVAIL. FOR EXAMPLE, BAGATELOS ARGUES THAT EX. D DEMONSTRATES THAT THE WINDOWS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE PRODUCT SPECIFICATIONS. (MOT., 8:13-21.) EX. D IS A PROJECT MANUAL THAT IS 1545 PAGES LONG. BAGALETOS DOES NOT HIGHLIGHT ANY PARTICULAR PORTION OF THAT DOCUMENT AND DID NOT EVEN PROVIDE A COPY OF IT TO THE COURT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED.

A PARTY ALLEGING AN ALTER EGO THEORY IS REQUIRED TO ALLEGE ONLY ULTIMATE RATHER THAN EVIDENTIARY FACTS. "MOREOVER, THE 'LESS PARTICULARITY [OF PLEADING] IS REQUIRED WHERE THE DEFENDANT MAY BE ASSUMED TO POSSESS KNOWLEDGE OF THE FACTS AT LEAST EQUAL, IF NOT SUPERIOR, TO THAT POSSESSED BY THE PLAINTIFF,' WHICH CERTAINLY IS THE CASE HERE." (RUTHERFORD HOLDINGS, LLC V. PLAZA DEL REY (2014) 223 CAL.APP.4TH 221, 235-236 [HOLDING THAT PLAINTIFF MADE SUFFICIENT ALLEGATIONS OF ALTER EGO TO AVOID A DEMURRER].) IN ADDITION, THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS DIRECT CLAIMS AGAINST ESWINDOWS AS ONE OF TWO CROSS-DEFENDANTS. (E.G., FACC PARAS. 8-14, 15-18, 36-39, 43-49, 72-73.). COUNSEL FOR BAGATELOS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Cross Defendant ESWindows, LLC'S Demurrer To Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s First Amended Cross Complaint as follows:

Cross-Defendant ESWindows, LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint is overruled. A party alleging an alter ego theory is required to allege only ultimate rather than evidentiary facts. "Moreover, the 'less particularity [of pleading] is required where the defendant may be assumed to possess knowledge of the facts at least equal, if not superior, to that possessed by the plaintiff,' which certainly is the case here." (Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 235-236 [holding that plaintiff made sufficient allegations of alter ego to avoid a demurrer].) In addition, the first amended cross-complaint adequately pleads direct claims against ESWindows as one of two cross-defendants. (E.g., FACC Paras. 8-14, 15-18, 36-39, 43-49, 72-73.)

Counsel for Bagatelos is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it to the court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A.'s DEMURRER TO Bagatelos Glass Systems Inc's 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant C.I. Energia Solar S.A.'s ("CIE") demurrer to Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s ("BAGS") amended cross-complaint is overruled in its entirety.

The breach of oral agreement cause of action alleges sufficient facts and is not uncertain. (See Amended Cross-Complaint, 8-11 and 28-29.) BAGS alleges that CIE provided non-conforming goods and services and given this stage of the litigation, CIE can reasonably respond to the amended complaint.

The fraud/misrepresentation claims are well-pleaded. BAGS identifies Carlos Amin as the person who made the representations and the discovery and summary adjudication motions that the parties have already litigated ought to have cleared up any uncertainty. The trial is set to be heard in a month.

The demurrer to the quantum meruit cause of action is overruled. (See Newport Harbor Ventures, LLC v. Morris Cerullo World Evangelism (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 1207, 1223 [noting that a party can plead inconsistent theories of recovery for breach of contract and quantum meruit].) Forcing BAGS to expressly allege that it is only pursuing the quantum meruit claim if the breach of contract claim fails is busy work. The court liberally reads the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure 452 and it is hornbook law that BAGS cannot simultaneously recover on both claims.

The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for disgorgement is overruled. The scope of CIE's work is a factual issue.

The demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled. The economic loss rule does not apply to negligently performed services. (See North American Chemical Co. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 764, 783-784.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT ESWINDOWS, LLC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons and Dismiss Complaint for lack of Jurisdiction.

Cross-defendant ESWindows, LLC's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is denied. The Court finds that ESWindows had sufficient minimum contacts with California for purposes of its exercising specific jurisdiction over it for Cross-Complainant Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s claims.

ESWindows directed its activities to the forum state by contracting with or at least performing a major part of the contract with Bagatelos. In particular, ESWindows provided the $1,500,938 written quote to Bagatelos "for ESWindows LLC to supply" the window and curtain wall systems (Rodriguez Decl. Exh. G; Archila Depo. 27-33); ESWindows' employees prepared specific designs for the project and were involved in their installation, including the project-specific shop drawings (Rodriguez Decl. J; Archila Depo. 75); the project manager for the project was an employee of ESWindows (Chamorro Depo. 35; Amin Depo. 110-11); the project was "a project of ESWindows, LLC" on which ESWindows employees worked (Archilla Depo. 31-32, 46-47; Subeh Depo. 19, 28-29), including participating in an on-site visit (Archila Depo. 210-213) and directing third parties working onsite (Subeh Depo. 28-29; 76-77); and ESWindows was involved with the booklet for the project (Vinas Depo Depo. 25-29; Chamorro Depo. 17-19).

All of Bagatelos' claims arise from ESWindows' conduct related to the project located in California, and ESWindows has not shown that exercise of the court's jurisdiction over it for the alleged acts of its employees directed at or in the forum state would be unreasonable. (See Luberski, Inc v. Oleificio F.lli Amato S.R.L. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 409, 419 [court had specific jurisdiction over Italian company that purposefully availed itself of California forum by contracting to deliver goods in California]; Magnecomp Corp. v. Athene Co. (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 526, 535 ["Since corporations by their nature can only operate through their appointed agents, courts consistently exercise personal jurisdiction over corporations based upon their agents' activities within the forum state."].)

Counsel for Bagatelos is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joe Abramson, Esq. 818-227-6690 for Plaintiff C.I.Energia through Court Call.
Chris Rodriguez, Esq. 916-646-8204 of Lewis Brisbois for Defendant BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., THE HANOVER INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the basis for the tentative ruling which was written after the recipt of the reply papers. Defendant argues, cites paragraph from Amin declaration, (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC.,, THE HANOVER INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Defendants and cross-complainants Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc. and The Hanover Insurance Company's (singularly and collectively referred to as BAGS) motion for summary adjudication on the first through third causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff and cross-defendant C.I. Energia Solar S.A, BAGS's third affirmative defense in its answer and on BAGS' sixth cause of action for disgorgement in its cross-complaint is denied in its entirety. BAGS fails to satisfy its initial summary adjudication burden. BAGS has not shown that CIE engaged in contracting work that required a license. The BAGS/Swinerton contract provides that BAGS was responsible for all engineering and preparation of all shop drawings. The CIE/BAGS contract further states that CIE "has no system design or application responsibility." Paragraphs 13-14 of the Amin declaration also shows that CIE was acting as a supplier within the meaning of Business and Professions Code 7045 and 7052 rather than a "contractor." There is evidence precluding summary adjudication that CIE merely created the window products and did not have any role in the installation process. There is also evidence that BAGS' employee (Stephen Fisher) consistently revised the shop drawings. CIE is similar to the supplier in Steinbrenner v. J.A. Waterbury Constr. Co. (1963) 212 Cal.App.2d 661 where the court concluded that a maker of custom cabinets for a building project was not a subcontractor for purposes of contractor licensing law when he did not install the cabinets. In this case, there is evidence that CIE fabricated the window products off site and BAGS performed all of the installation work.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A.'S Motion For Order Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 426.50 And 428.50 Granting Plaintiff Leave To File A New Cross-Complaint Naming Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc, And New Cross-Defendants is granted. Although granting the motion will likely necessitate continuing the trial, granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. This policy is particularly apt where, as here, some of the new claims sought to be added are compulsory cross-claims. None of defendant and cross-complainant Bagatelos Glass Systems, Inc.'s arguments regarding the insufficiency or lack of merit of the proposed new claims against it appear well-founded, certainly not as clearly meritorious as its economic loss argument was with regard to the Energia Solar's prior motion for leave to file a cross-complaint. In all events, granting this motion does not preclude Bagatelos from demurring to any or all of the new claims against it, if it believes in good faith that it has good grounds to do so. Counsel for Energia Solar and Bagatelos are invited to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a new trial date that would accommodate the likely needs of the two new parties will be added to the case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 428.50 Granting Plaintiff Leave To File A Permissive Cross-Complaint Naming New Cross-Defendants; Declaration Of Joe R. Abramson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOE R. ABRAMSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF JOE R. ABRAMSON, TEL. NO. 1-818-227-6690 EXT. 300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER R. RODRIGUEZ OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH, TEL. NO. 1-916-646-8204 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A'S, Motion For Order Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 428.50 Granting Plaintiff Leave To File A Permissive Cross-Complaint Naming New Cross-Defendants - Cross-defendant C.I. Energia Solar S.A.'s motion for leave to file a permissive cross-complaint naming new cross-defendants is denied without prejudice to bringing another motion based on a proposed cross-complaint that alleges claims not barred by the economic loss rule. All claims alleged in the proposed cross-complaint that is the basis for this motion are barred by the economic loss rule. None of the purported exceptions to that rule that Energia Solar asserts in its reply memorandum are applicable. Because the proposed cross-claims are for indemnity, they are derivative of cross-complainant Bagatelos Glass System's, Inc.'s cross-claims, none of which allege property damage and thus the property damage "exception" to the economic loss rule is inapplicable. Nor do any of the proposed cross-claims adequately allege a "special relationship" between Energia Solar and the proposed new cross-defendants. Energia Solar's argument that the economic loss rule does not apply to services lacks merit. Northern American Chemical Company v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal. App. 4th 764, 785, the sole case that it cites for that proposition, merely states that the special relationship criteria apply when a contract for services is involved. If it can do so in good faith, Energia Solar may bring another motion seeking leave to file a proposed cross-complaint based on claims not barred by the economic loss rule, which would not include any indemnity claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
